data.title: Trump's Ceasefire Proposal Includes Israel Amid Hormuz Strait Concerns
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has advanced a ceasefire proposal that includes Israel, according to BlockBeats. The plan stipulates that one of the conditions for the ceasefire is the resumption of navigation through the Hormuz Strait.

Meanwhile, Israel has reported intercepting missile attacks from Iran, indicating that regional conflicts continue to escalate.

The current situation reveals that although there has been some progress in ceasefire negotiations, the primary disagreement remains centered on the passage through the Hormuz Strait, which is a crucial factor affecting the implementation of the ceasefire.

data.title: CME FedWatch Tool Indicates Low Probability of April Rate Hike
data.description: The CME FedWatch Tool reveals a 1.6% probability of a 25 basis point rate hike by the Federal Reserve in April. According to ChainCatcher, there is a 98.4% likelihood that the rates will remain unchanged. By June, the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut is 0%, while the chance of maintaining the current rates is 97.9%. The likelihood of a cumulative 25 basis point rate increase stands at 2.1%.

data.title: White House official: Israel has also agreed to a temporary ceasefire.
data.description: White House officials say Israel has also agreed to a temporary ceasefire. (CNN)

data.title: STOCKS | Nasdaq Futures Rise 2% as Middle East Tensions Ease
data.description: Nasdaq futures have expanded their gains to 2% following a decrease in tensions in the Middle East. According to Jin10, this development has also led to a significant drop in WTI crude oil prices, which have plummeted by 11%, falling below the $100 mark. Meanwhile, gold prices have increased by 1.6%.

data.title: US President Trump: I have agreed to a two-week halt to bombing and attacks on Iran; this will be a two-way ceasefire.
data.description: US President Trump: I have agreed to a two-week halt to bombing and attacks on Iran, which will be a two-way ceasefire. We have made great progress on a final plan for a long-term peace agreement with Iran. (Jinshi)

data.title: Pakistan's Prime Minister Proposes Two-Week Plan for U.S.-Iran Talks
data.description: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has proposed a 'two-week plan' to facilitate negotiations between the United States and Iran. According to BlockBeats, a regional source indicated that positive developments are anticipated soon, with the talks being directly led by Pakistan's Army Chief, General Asim Munir. An agreement is expected to be finalized by tonight, local time.

data.title: Federal Reserve Vice Chair Jefferson Discusses Interest Rate Preparedness
data.description: Federal Reserve Vice Chair Jefferson has stated that the current policy interest rate is well-prepared to address market changes. According to ChainCatcher, Jefferson noted that the rate is approximately in a neutral range.

data.title: Federal Reserve Vice Chair Warns of Impact of High Energy Prices
data.description: Federal Reserve Vice Chair Jefferson has expressed concerns about the effects of persistently high energy prices. According to ChainCatcher, Jefferson stated that elevated energy costs could negatively impact consumer and business spending. This warning highlights the potential economic challenges posed by rising energy expenses.

data.title: Federal Reserve Vice Chair Comments on Employment Growth Stabilization
data.description: Federal Reserve Vice Chair Jefferson has noted that while employment growth has slowed, it may be stabilizing. According to ChainCatcher, Jefferson's remarks highlight the current state of the labor market, suggesting a potential stabilization despite recent declines in job growth.

data.title: Fed Vice Chair Jefferson: U.S. Inflation Remains Above Target
data.description: U.S. Federal Reserve Vice Chair Philip Jefferson stated that the country's inflation rate continues to exceed the central bank's target. According to Jin10, Jefferson emphasized the ongoing challenges in bringing inflation down to the desired level. He noted that while there have been some improvements, the inflation rate remains a concern for policymakers. Jefferson's comments highlight the Federal Reserve's focus on achieving price stability as part of its broader economic objectives. The central bank continues to monitor economic indicators closely to determine the appropriate monetary policy actions needed to address inflationary pressures.

data.title: U.S. Lawmakers Urge CFTC Action on Insider Trading in Prediction Markets
data.description:
Seven members of the United States House of Representatives have addressed a letter to Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) Chair Michael Selig, seeking clarity on the agency's approach to insider trading within prediction markets and event contracts linked to war and conflicts. According to Cointelegraph, the lawmakers emphasized the CFTC's authority under the Commodities Exchange Act to enforce rules aimed at preventing evasion of the act's swap provisions. This assertion supports Selig's stance that the commission holds jurisdiction over prediction markets.

The representatives voiced concerns regarding the CFTC's oversight of event contracts deemed morally questionable, particularly those involving U.S. military actions in Iran and Venezuela. These contracts have reportedly seen suspicious trading activities tied to the timing and outcomes of military engagements. The letter stressed the need for prompt and decisive regulatory action, warning that the persistence of such contracts raises doubts about the commission's commitment and capability to fulfill its global regulatory responsibilities.

Legal challenges surrounding the regulation of prediction market platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket are unfolding at both federal and state levels. Several U.S. state gaming authorities have initiated lawsuits, accusing these companies of illegally offering sports bets. Meanwhile, the CFTC, under Selig's leadership, maintains that the event contracts on these platforms qualify as swaps and are subject to federal regulations. The House members have requested Selig to address six specific questions by April 15.

In a recent legal development, the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Third Circuit upheld a lower court's decision preventing New Jersey gaming authorities from pursuing enforcement actions against Kalshi. Two of the three circuit judges concluded that the company has a reasonable chance of success in arguing that federal commodities laws supersede state regulations.

The letter from the lawmakers follows remarks by CFTC enforcement director David Miller, who addressed concerns about insider trading. Miller indicated that the commission would prosecute cases involving individuals who trade with misappropriated information but would not allocate resources to minor infractions. This stance aligns with legislative proposals from Democrats aimed at addressing insider trading issues.

data.title: U.S. API Distillate Stocks Decline by 599,000 Barrels
data.description: According to Jin10, the American Petroleum Institute (API) reported that U.S. distillate inventories decreased by 599,000 barrels for the week ending April 3. This figure was below the expected decline of 869,000 barrels and follows a previous decrease of 1.043 million barrels.

data.title: Iran Ready for Peace or Conflict, Says Senior Official
data.description: A senior Iranian official has stated that Iran is prepared for both peace and conflict. According to NS3.AI, this declaration underscores the country's readiness to navigate various geopolitical scenarios. The official emphasized Iran's strategic positioning and its ability to respond to different international developments. This statement comes amid ongoing tensions in the region, highlighting Iran's stance on maintaining its sovereignty and security.

data.title: U.S. API Gasoline Inventory Decline Exceeds Expectations
data.description: The American Petroleum Institute (API) reported a significant decrease in U.S. gasoline inventories for the week ending April 3. According to Jin10, the gasoline stockpiles fell by 3.968 million barrels, surpassing the anticipated decline of 1.409 million barrels. This reduction also exceeded the previous week's decrease of 3.209 million barrels. The larger-than-expected drop in gasoline inventories may impact market dynamics and fuel prices.

data.title: Pimco Considers $14 Billion Debt Financing for Oracle Data Center
data.description: Pacific Investment Management Company (Pimco) is reportedly contemplating a $14 billion debt financing deal for Oracle's data center, according to sources familiar with the matter. If finalized, Pimco would become a key supporter of Oracle's data center campus located in Saline, Michigan. Oracle plans to use the facility to provide services for OpenAI applications. Sources indicate that the financing might be structured as bonds, with Pimco potentially selling a portion of these bonds to other investors.

data.title: Sky Governance Proposes Upgrades to Enhance Sky Protocol's Stability
data.description: Sky Governance has put forward two structural upgrades aimed at bolstering the capital protection framework of Sky Protocol. According to NS3.AI, the proposal includes the introduction of a more robust solvency buffer and a sustainable staking rewards model. These measures are designed to enhance the long-term stability of the protocol.

data.title: Japan Expands Tax-Free Savings Accounts Amid Youth Investment Concerns
data.description: Japan has significantly increased the scope of its tax-free savings accounts, raising concerns about the investment behavior of its younger population. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that some experts worry that the youth are experiencing a fear of missing out (FOMO) as they seek higher returns. This expansion aims to encourage savings and investment among the Japanese population, but it also raises questions about the potential risks associated with inexperienced investors entering the market. The move is part of a broader strategy to stimulate economic growth and financial literacy in the country.
